fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Oklahoma Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Oklahoma - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity oklahoma, how to transfer audiologist license to oklahoma, audiologist compact oklahoma, oklahoma audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Examiners for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
board.parent_dept: Oklahoma Board of Examiners for SLP and Audiology
board.mailing_address:
3000 North Stiles Ave., Suite 203
Oklahoma City, OK 73105
board.physical_address: 3700 N. Classen Blvd., Suite 248, Oklahoma City, OK 73118

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in Oklahoma.

steps.title: Register and Initiate Application
steps.description: Create an account on the Oklahoma Licensing Portal (licensing.ok.gov), log in, and begin the Audiologist license application.

steps.title: Complete Application & Upload Documents
steps.description: Fill out the online application completely and upload all required documents, including official transcripts and verification of ASHA CCC-A or ABAA certification.

steps.title: Arrange for Third-Party Submissions
steps.description: Request official transcripts to be sent directly from your educational institution to OBESPA, and ensure your Praxis Examination score report is sent directly from ETS to OBESPA.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee of $100 through the Oklahoma Licensing Portal.

steps.title: Submit Application for Review
steps.description: Electronically submit your completed application and monitor its status in the Oklahoma Licensing Portal. An initial license fee of $100 will be billed upon approval, and a fingerprint background check fee of $43.25 is also required.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Oklahoma audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Oklahoma have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Oklahoma participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Oklahoma without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Oklahoma?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Oklahoma while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.dek: To apply for an Audiologist license in Oklahoma, create an account and submit your application through the Oklahoma Licensing Portal.
